flashcards.Back: The Cisco global command used to set a highly secure, hashed password for accessing privileged EXEC mode. it takes precedence over the legacy 'enable password' command.
The command to enter configuration mode for the physical serial console port, used for local out-of-band management access.
The command to enter configuration mode for the 16 virtual terminal lines, which support remote network management connections like Telnet and SSH.
A line configuration subcommand that instructs the device to prompt for both a username and a password, verified against the local user database.
A global configuration command that applies a weak (Type 7) encryption to all clear-text passwords in the configuration file to prevent shoulder surfing.
The global command used to create a local user account. The 'secret' keyword ensures the password is stored as a secure SHA-256 or Scrypt hash.
The administrative mode on a Cisco device (indicated by the # prompt) that allows for full configuration and monitoring. access is usually protected by an enable secret.
The default starting mode on a Cisco device (indicated by the > prompt) with limited, non-disruptive monitoring capabilities.
A one-way mathematical process that converts a plain-text password into a fixed-length string. The original password cannot be easily reversed from the hash.
A password stored in its original, readable form. These are insecure and can be seen by anyone with access to the configuration file or network sniffer.
A secure hashing algorithm (MD5) used by Cisco for the 'enable secret' command in older IOS versions.
A weak, reversible encryption used by 'service password-encryption'. it is intended only to hide passwords from casual viewing.
Modern, highly secure hashing algorithms (SHA-256 and Scrypt) used in newer Cisco IOS versions for storing local user and enable secret passwords.
A line configuration command used to specify which protocols are allowed for remote access. 'transport input ssh' is the most secure setting.
A line configuration command used to set the interval of inactivity after which the device automatically disconnects a management session.
A subcommand that enables password checking on a line. without this (or 'login local'), the device may allow access without any password at all.
The subcommand used to set a simple, non-username password for access to a specific line (Console or VTY).
A table of usernames and hashed passwords stored directly in the device's running configuration, used for local authentication.
A framework that can be enabled on Cisco devices to provide more advanced access control, often using external servers like RADIUS or TACACS+.
A line configuration command used to apply an IP Access Control List to the VTY lines, restricting remote management to specific subnets.
The command used to verify the current device configuration, including checking that passwords are encrypted and lines are secured.
The original Cisco command for securing privileged access. it is less secure because it stores the password in plain text by default.
Administrative access to a device that does not rely on the network data path, such as using the physical console port.
Administrative access to a device that occurs over the network data path, such as using SSH across a production VLAN.
A physical port on many Cisco routers designed for remote dial-up modem access. it can be secured with passwords similar to the console port.
Configuring a local user account with 'privilege 15' allows that user to enter privileged EXEC mode immediately upon logging in via SSH/Console.
Checking that a legal warning message is displayed upon login to satisfy corporate security and compliance requirements.
A configuration that prevents log messages from interrupting an administrator's command input, making management easier.
The resistance of a hashed password to being cracked.SHA-256 based 'secrets' are significantly stronger than MD5 or plain-text passwords.
A security scenario where an administrator loses all passwords to a device and must perform a physical password recovery process.
